articles.title: Bullion watch: Gold falls over 2%, silver drops nearly 4% as crude prices soar to $100
Oil holds above $100 as Red Sea attacks and Kazakhstan outage tighten supplies
articles.summary: Oil prices have sharply rebounded, exceeding one hundred dollars per barrel. Attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea have revived supply fears. Kazakhstan's oil exports also faced disruptions, adding to global concerns.

articles.title: IndiGo offers crisis management plan to settle CCI probe into flight disruption case
articles.summary: IndiGo has proposed commitments to the Competition Commission of India. These proposals aim to address concerns over past flight disruptions and unfair practices. The airline will establish a crisis management group and a passenger grievance mechanism.

articles.title: 'Failure is okay, cheating is not': Sachin Tendulkar recalls father's lesson amid NEET agitation
articles.summary: Amid growing student protests over alleged irregularities in the education system, Tendulkar urged society to ensure that young people never feel their efforts have gone unrewarded.

articles.title: US stocks today: Wall Street trades in red as oil tops $100 per barrel; Nasdaq tumbles over 2%, Dow slips over 600 points
articles.summary: Major stock markets opened lower as Big Tech's AI spending raised investor concerns. Alphabet and Tesla shares fell significantly after their latest quarterly earnings reports. Oil prices surged past one hundred dollars per barrel amid Middle East tensions.

articles.title: Premium petrol to remain ethanol-free; no plan to blend beyond 20% as of now: Govt to Parliament
articles.summary: Premium petrol grades will continue to be ethanol-free for consumers. The government has no plans to reintroduce lower ethanol blends like E0 or E10. Current ethanol blending in regular petrol will remain at twenty percent.

articles.summary: Expected to boost exports, benefit global e-commerce players like Amazon

articles.summary: Exporters are also looking forward to the relief from the expiry of tariffs.

articles.title: Oil's $100 comeback: Brent hits a three-month high as Red Sea tensions escalate
articles.summary: Crude oil prices surged past one hundred dollars per barrel amid Middle East tensions. Houthi attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea caused market concern. This escalation has heightened fears over global energy supply safety and stability.

articles.title: Pay10 launches in Bahrain, expands digital payments business with consumer, merchant apps
articles.summary: Pay10 has formally entered Bahrain's digital payments market with consumer and merchant payment platforms, expanding its footprint in the Gulf region. The company said its offerings are designed to support Bahrain's cashless economy through secure, interoperable payment services.

articles.title: 'I chose work over my grandfather's last rites': Indian woman in US Says startup laid her off before issuing offer letter
articles.summary: After being assured that her updated offer letter would be shared soon, the employee says she waited for nearly three months before receiving a layoff call instead.

articles.title: Infosys to roll out salary hikes in three phases from October; senior employees to get increments in January
articles.summary: Infosys will begin rolling out annual salary hikes from October in a three-phase process, with most employees receiving increments in October and senior employees getting theirs in January.

articles.title: Why have three law firms launched a probe against HDFC Bank?
articles.summary: According to the US law firms, following a news report on May 27, 2026, HDFC Bank’s stock price declined $1.02, or 4.1%, to close at $23.78; they called upon investors who suffered a loss to contact them over potentially pursuing claims.

articles.summary: The new technology arm will focus on delivering enterprise digital transformation, systems integration, smart infrastructure, mission-critical facilities, and data centers.

articles.title: India's first bullet train will detect earthquakes and stop automatically — here's the full safety system explained
articles.summary: Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train electrification is underway — 2×25 kV system, 22,000 steel masts, 28 earthquake sensors and Made-in-India components powering India's first 320 kmph corridor.

articles.title: Headline inflation rises, but RBI says core inflation remains subdued. What's the difference?
articles.summary: The RBI's latest Bulletin says headline retail inflation edged higher in June, but underlying price pressures remained subdued as core inflation stayed low.

articles.title: Who is Abhijeet Dipke? Meet the journalism graduate and Boston University alumnus behind the Cockroach Janta Party
articles.summary: Dipke first shot to national prominence in May this year after controversial remarks linked to Chief Justice Surya Kant triggered outrage among students and unemployed youth.

articles.title: Longer routes, higher costs, 26,000 cancellations: US-Iran conflict hits Indian airlines
articles.summary: Indian airlines have cancelled approximately 26,000 international flights due to Middle East airspace restrictions. These disruptions have led to flight diversions and delays, impacting airline operations significantly.

articles.title: Will employees finally get paid faster after resignation? Understanding labour codes and full & final settlement
articles.summary: For an employee, the last salary from the previous organisation is not just a payroll entry. It may be needed for rent, relocation expenses, family commitments, education fees, loan repayments or simply to manage the gap before the first salary from the new employer

articles.title: India's first hydrogen-powered train clocks around 900 km in five days
articles.summary: India's first hydrogen fuel cell train has covered 900 kilometers on its initial operational route. This new train runs on hydrogen fuel cells, producing only water vapor and heat. Passenger fares remain consistent with ordinary diesel services on this pilot project.

articles.title: Who is Ashiss Kumar Dash, the new CEO-designate at IT major Infosys?
articles.summary: Ashiss Kumar Dash currently serves as the global head of a diversified business portfolio spanning more than 12 industry verticals.

articles.title: Axis Bank's MF arm acquires Rs 12,000 crore PMS business from securities arm
articles.summary: Axis Asset Management Company is acquiring Axis Securities' portfolio management services business. This strategic move significantly expands their alternative investments platform and investor base.

articles.title: Beyond Hormuz: Gulf is spending billions to find new routes to global markets
articles.summary: Gulf nations are accelerating pipeline projects to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. These new routes aim to move oil without relying on the narrow waterway. Several major pipeline projects are under construction or being planned.

articles.title: Rupee rises to 96.49 against US dollar, rising oil prices limit gains
articles.summary: The Indian rupee saw a slight gain against the US dollar on Thursday. Rising crude oil prices continue to exert pressure on the currency. The Reserve Bank of India's intervention is expected to limit sharper losses.
